WorkOS
workos.com в†—

WorkOS provides the enterprise auth features (SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, audit logs, directory sync) that startups need to sell upmarket вЂ” without building any of it themselves. The engineering culture is small-team, deeply technical, and famously well-written. The interview is selective and rewards engineers with strong written communication.

Process

Recruiter screen в†’ take-home async work sample (paid) в†’ 60-minute pair-programming в†’ 60-minute system design в†’ 60-minute past-project deep dive в†’ behavioral. Cycle: 3вЂ“5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a SAML SSO integration that handles arbitrary IdP idiosyncrasies (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace)

- Design SCIM provisioning with bidirectional sync and conflict handling

- Design an audit log API with immutable append and queryable retention

- Coding: practical Node/TypeScript, often involving XML or JSON parsing

- Past-project deep dive: defend choices and articulate tradeoffs

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $180KвЂ“$240K total

- Senior SE: $260KвЂ“$340K

- Staff: $360KвЂ“$470K

- Principal: $490KвЂ“$640K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript and at least one auth protocol (SAML, OIDC, SCIM)

- Read the WorkOS engineering blog вЂ” they hire people who already engage

- Have a strong portfolio of writing вЂ” blog posts, RFCs, OSS contributions

Frequently Asked Questions

Is WorkOS fully remote?

Yes. ~80 employees globally with concentration in North America. Quarterly off-sites are optional.

How does WorkOS compare to Auth0 (Okta) or Clerk?

Auth0/Okta is broader and more enterprise. Clerk is consumer-app-friendly. WorkOS is laser-focused on the B2B "make my SaaS enterprise-ready" niche.

What is the take-home like?

Realistic and bounded вЂ” usually 3вЂ“4 hours, paid. Quality of writing matters as much as code.
